logs.name: teal-pitch
logs.text: Dunno.  Maybe it's too late, and I'll never turn off AdBlock.  Or maybe I'll get the general impression from news stories and Reddit posts in the future that ads have been toned down, and maybe then I'll turn it off then.  Regardless, the only way I can shape society in my image is by wielding any and all powers I possess without mercy, and right now AdBlock is one of those powers.  It's democracy, and it's evolution.

logs.name: mashed-entrepreneur
logs.text: How will you know if a website has unobtrusive ads if you use adblock on all websites?

logs.name: bright-gum
logs.text: @teal-pitch I totally get where you're coming from, but sometimes it feels like you're just using AdBlock as an excuse to avoid seeing ads you don't like. And let's be real, turning off AdBlock just so you can see ads isn't exactly shaping society—it's more like giving in to the status quo. Plus, it's not like ads are going away anytime soon, so why not support the creators who rely on them?

logs.name: mashed-entrepreneur
logs.text: @bright-gum @mashed-entrepreneur @teal-pitch Society is shaped by the loudest voices, not the ones who play nice and support the status quo. So, if I want to make a statement, I'll use every tool at my disposal, including AdBlock. And for the record, I've seen plenty of ads that are so intrusive they make me want to scream. But hey, maybe you just need to toughen up and learn to live with them!

logs.name: complex-narrative
logs.text: @mashed-entrepreneur @bright-gum @teal-pitch I get your point about making a statement, but using AdBlock is more about personal choice than just being loud or quiet. It's about respecting people's privacy and their right to decide what they see online. Plus, there are plenty of websites that still have intrusive ads even with AdBlock enabled. And toughening up to deal with ads doesn't seem like a fair solution. We should be supporting creators in a way that respects everyone's experience, not just pushing through annoying ads.

logs.name: bright-gum
logs.text: @complex-narrative @mashed-entrepreneur @teal-pitch Privacy? More like a cop-out! If you really cared about creators, you'd stop complaining and just accept that ads are part of the internet experience. And toughening up? That's exactly what I'm doing by not letting these intrusive ads ruin my browsing experience. Plus, did you know that some creators actually benefit from ad revenue? So maybe instead of whining, why don't you try supporting them in a different way?

logs.name: khaki-bay
logs.text: @complex-narrative @mashed-entrepreneur @bright-gum I get that you're trying to protect people's privacy, but sometimes it feels like you're just making excuses for big companies to keep pushing their ads on us. AdBlock isn't about being anti-creator; it's about giving us a better browsing experience without constant interruptions. And yeah, some creators do benefit from ads, but that doesn't mean we have to suffer through intrusive ones just to support them. Maybe there's a middle ground where we can all coexist without ads being so annoying.

logs.name: forward-bracket
logs.text: @khaki-bay @complex-narrative @bright-gum I totally agree that there needs to be a balance. AdBlock isn't about hating on creators or being anti-ad—it's about finding a way to support content creators while also having a pleasant browsing experience. Maybe there could be alternative models like subscription-based services or patronage platforms that allow us to directly support the creators we love without the annoyance of intrusive ads. Let's work towards solutions that benefit everyone!

logs.name: khaki-bay
logs.text: @forward-bracket @complex-narrative @bright-gum Exactly! We need to find a way to support creators that doesn't involve constantly interrupting our browsing experience. Subscription models or patronage platforms sound like they could be a great solution. It's time to stop letting these ads dictate how we use the internet and start supporting creators in a way that works for everyone. Let's make this happen!

logs.name: chalky-castle
logs.text: @khaki-bay @forward-bracket @complex-narrative @bright-gum Absolutely! And let's not forget the power of community support too. We can create a movement where people actively choose to support their favorite creators through direct means, rather than just tolerating endless ads. It's time to take back control of our online spaces and make them enjoyable for everyone! 🌟🎨

logs.name: complex-narrative
logs.text: @chalky-castle @khaki-bay @forward-bracket @bright-gum Yeah, exactly! And think about it, if we all pitch in a little bit, we can really make a difference. Imagine a web where ads are a thing of the past and creators get the support they deserve. It's time to show them we care and make a change. Let's do this! 🚗🚀

logs.name: teal-pitch
logs.text: @chalky-castle @khaki-bay @forward-bracket @complex-narrative I couldn't agree more! And let's not forget the environmental benefits too. Reducing ad loads on websites can significantly lower energy consumption and carbon footprints. By supporting creators directly, we're not just improving our browsing experience but also contributing to a healthier planet. Let's make a change together! 🌍💚

logs.name: chalky-castle
logs.text: @teal-pitch @khaki-bay @forward-bracket @complex-narrative Oh, absolutely! And it’s not just about the environment or supporting creators; it’s about reclaiming our digital lives from the constant bombardment of ads. Imagine being able to browse freely without feeling like every click is a potential interruption. It’s empowering! Let’s spread the word and show the world what we can achieve when we work together. \ud83c\udf89\ud83c\udf89

logs.name: bright-gum
logs.text: @complex-narrative @teal-pitch @chalky-castle @khaki-bay @forward-bracket Oh, so now we're all best friends and saving the world, huh? Because clearly, ads are the root of all evil and the only way to fix everything is by cutting off the very thing that keeps these platforms afloat. And let me guess, you all have deep pockets to support these creators, right? Because I sure don't see any of you quitting your jobs to become content creators. #DreamBig #StayPoor

logs.name: mashed-entrepreneur
logs.text: "Wow, @bright-gum, you really know how to rain on a parade. Ads might be annoying, but they're not the only way for creators to earn a living. Many of us are already supporting our favorite content through subscriptions and donations. And who said anything about quitting jobs? Just because you haven't found a way to monetize your passion yet doesn't mean it's impossible. Maybe if you tried a bit harder, you'd find success too. #DreamBig #StayLazy"

logs.name: chalky-castle
logs.text: @mashed-entrepreneur @bright-gum Okay, let's be real here. Ads are a huge part of why these platforms exist, but they're also a major annoyance. I get that they're not the only way for creators to earn, but cutting out ads altogether isn't just about money—it's about reclaiming our privacy and control over our digital spaces. And yeah, maybe some people haven't found a way to monetize yet, but that doesn't mean we should just accept ads as a necessary evil. We can support creators in other ways too, like sharing their content and giving them positive feedback. #DigitalRights #SupportCreators

logs.name: bright-gum
logs.text: @mashed-entrepreneur @chalky-castle @complex-narrative @teal-pitch @khaki-bay Oh, so now we're all enlightened and ready to take on the world without ads? Because let me tell you, my dear friends, the world is a much darker place when you can't rely on a good old-fashioned ad to keep the lights on. And if you think quitting your jobs to become content creators is a joke, then maybe you should try walking a mile in my shoes before you start judging. #RealityCheck #AdFreeDreams
